questions.question: What scheduling algorithm assigns the CPU to the process with the smallest burst time?
questions.explanation: SJF minimizes average waiting time by always scheduling the process with the shortest estimated CPU burst next.

questions.question: What is a race condition?
options: When two processes run at the same speed
When two processes access shared data concurrently and the outcome depends on execution order
When the CPU runs out of processes to execute
When a process waits indefinitely for a resource
questions.explanation: A race condition occurs when multiple threads access shared data without synchronization, and the final result depends on the unpredictable execution order.

questions.question: Which OS data structure stores all information about a running process?
questions.explanation: The PCB (Process Control Block) stores the process state, program counter, registers, memory info, and scheduling information.
